# Test edge cases and error handling

# Test missing binary
! exec cmd2mcpserver nonexistent-binary
stderr 'Binary not found'

# Test invalid flags combination (no binary)
! exec cmd2mcpserver
stderr 'Usage:'

# Test with custom naming
exec go build -o mycli testcli.go
! stderr .
exec cmd2mcpserver -tool custom-name -server CustomServer -dry-run ./mycli
stdout 'type CustomServerTool struct'
stdout '"custom-name"'

# Test with FlagVar functions
exec go build -o varcli varcli.go
! stderr .
exec cmd2mcpserver -dry-run -source . ./varcli
stdout '"name"'
stdout '"port"'
stdout '"enabled"'
stdout '"rate"'

-- testcli.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	test := flag.String("test", "default", "A test flag")
	flag.Parse()
	fmt.Println(*test)
}

-- varcli.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
)

var (
	name    string
	port    int
	enabled bool
	rate    float64
)

func main() {
	flag.StringVar(&name, "name", "default", "Name parameter")
	flag.IntVar(&port, "port", 8080, "Port number")
	flag.BoolVar(&enabled, "enabled", false, "Enable feature")
	flag.Float64Var(&rate, "rate", 1.0, "Rate limit")
	flag.Parse()
	fmt.Printf("name=%s port=%d enabled=%v rate=%f\n", name, port, enabled, rate)
}
